# INVEST IN YOUR HOME, WITH HYTEK ROOFING.

## HYTEK ROOFING

# **Frequently Asked**

# **Questions**

## General Roof Plumbing

#### What is roof plumbing?

Roof plumbing involves installing, repairing, and maintaining gutters, downpipes, flashings, and roof drainage systems. It ensures water flows away from your roof and foundations, protecting your home from leaks and structural damage.

#### Do I need a licensed roof plumber in Brisbane?

Yes. In Queensland, the QBCC requires all roof plumbing work (including gutters and downpipes) to be carried out by a licensed professional. Using a licensed roof plumber guarantees your work meets Australian Standards and council regulations.

#### How often should I service my roof plumbing?

We recommend a professional inspection at least once a year, ideally before Brisbane’s summer storm season. If you live near trees, consider inspections every 3–6 months.

## Gutters & Downpipes

#### How do I know if my gutters need replacing?

You may need new gutters if you notice:

\- Sagging or pulling away from the roofline

\- Rust or visible holes

\- Overflow during rain

\- Water pooling around your foundation

#### Can I replace gutters or downpipes myself?

No. In Brisbane, gutter and downpipe replacement must be completed by a licensed roof plumber to comply with the National Construction Code (NCC) and QBCC standards.

#### What size gutters are right for Brisbane’s heavy rainfall?

Every property is different. Gutters and downpipes are sized according to AS/NZS 3500.3 stormwater standards, taking into account roof area and local rainfall intensity. Our team ensures your system can handle even the heaviest Brisbane storms.

## Roof Leaks & Flashings

#### What causes roof leaks in Brisbane homes?

Common causes include:

\- Blocked or overflowing gutters

\- Rusted or damaged flashings

\- Cracked roof sheets or tiles

\- Storm damage or poor installation

#### Do flashings fall under roof plumbing?

Yes. Flashings seal gaps around skylights, chimneys, and roof joints. Damaged or poorly installed flashings are one of the top causes of leaks.

#### Should flashings be replaced during re-roofing?

Yes. When re-roofing, we always install new flashings to ensure the roof is fully watertight and compliant with current codes.

## Stormwater & Drainage

#### Where should my downpipes drain in Brisbane?

Downpipes must connect to an approved stormwater system such as:

\- The street kerb

\- Underground stormwater drainage

\- An onsite stormwater pit

#### Can roof water be directed into the sewer?

No. Queensland regulations require stormwater to remain separate from sewerage systems.

#### What if my property floods from poor drainage?

A licensed roof plumber can assess your current setup, redesign the system, and ensure compliance with Brisbane City Council requirements.

## Maintenance

## & Compliance

#### What regulations apply to roof plumbing in Brisbane?

All work must comply with:

\- NCC (National Construction Code, Volume 2 – Housing Provisions)

\- AS/NZS 3500.3: Plumbing & Drainage – Stormwater

\- QBCC licensing requirements

\- Brisbane City Council guidelines

#### How can I prevent blocked gutters?

Regular gutter cleaning, trimming back trees, and installing gutter guards are the best ways to avoid costly blockages.

#### Do roof plumbers also install rainwater tanks?

Yes. We can connect your roof plumbing to a rainwater harvesting system, helping you save water and meet Brisbane City Council sustainability requirements.

## Need a Roof Plumber

## In Brisbane?

Whether it’s gutter repairs, stormwater drainage, roof leak prevention, or a full replacement, Hytek Roofing is here to help.

Call us today for expert roof plumbing services in Brisbane — done once, done right.

# **Frequently Asked**

# **Questions**

## General Roof Plumbing

#### What is roof plumbing?

Roof plumbing involves installing, repairing, and maintaining gutters, downpipes, flashings, and roof drainage systems. It ensures water flows away from your roof and foundations, protecting your home from leaks and structural damage.

#### Do I need a licensed roof plumber in Brisbane?

Yes. In Queensland, the QBCC requires all roof plumbing work (including gutters and downpipes) to be carried out by a licensed professional. Using a licensed roof plumber guarantees your work meets Australian Standards and council regulations.

#### How often should I service my roof plumbing?

We recommend a professional inspection at least once a year, ideally before Brisbane’s summer storm season. If you live near trees, consider inspections every 3–6 months.

## Gutters & Downpipes

#### How do I know if my gutters need replacing?

You may need new gutters if you notice:

\- Sagging or pulling away from the roofline

\- Rust or visible holes

\- Overflow during rain

\- Water pooling around your foundation

#### Can I replace gutters or downpipes myself?

No. In Brisbane, gutter and downpipe replacement must be completed by a licensed roof plumber to comply with the National Construction Code (NCC) and QBCC standards.

#### What size gutters are right for Brisbane’s heavy rainfall?

Every property is different. Gutters and downpipes are sized according to AS/NZS 3500.3 stormwater standards, taking into account roof area and local rainfall intensity. Our team ensures your system can handle even the heaviest Brisbane storms.

## Roof Leaks & Flashings

#### What causes roof leaks in Brisbane homes?

Common causes include:

\- Blocked or overflowing gutters

\- Rusted or damaged flashings

\- Cracked roof sheets or tiles

\- Storm damage or poor installation

#### Do flashings fall under roof plumbing?

Yes. Flashings seal gaps around skylights, chimneys, and roof joints. Damaged or poorly installed flashings are one of the top causes of leaks.

#### Should flashings be replaced during re-roofing?

Yes. When re-roofing, we always install new flashings to ensure the roof is fully watertight and compliant with current codes.

## Stormwater & Drainage

#### Where should my downpipes drain in Brisbane?

Downpipes must connect to an approved stormwater system such as:

\- The street kerb

\- Underground stormwater drainage

\- An onsite stormwater pit

#### Can roof water be directed into the sewer?

No. Queensland regulations require stormwater to remain separate from sewerage systems.

#### What if my property floods from poor drainage?

A licensed roof plumber can assess your current setup, redesign the system, and ensure compliance with Brisbane City Council requirements.

## Maintenance

## & Compliance

#### What regulations apply to roof plumbing in Brisbane?

All work must comply with:

\- NCC (National Construction Code, Volume 2 – Housing Provisions)

\- AS/NZS 3500.3: Plumbing & Drainage – Stormwater

\- QBCC licensing requirements

\- Brisbane City Council guidelines

#### How can I prevent blocked gutters?

Regular gutter cleaning, trimming back trees, and installing gutter guards are the best ways to avoid costly blockages.

#### Do roof plumbers also install rainwater tanks?

Yes. We can connect your roof plumbing to a rainwater harvesting system, helping you save water and meet Brisbane City Council sustainability requirements.
